name: openai
context: OpenAI platform — chat completions, embeddings, image generation, assistants, and function calling
info: The OpenAI ecosystem covers AI model integration via the OpenAI API platform. Primary capabilities include chat completions (GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o1, o3), text embeddings for vector search and RAG, image generation and editing with DALL-E, persistent assistants with threads and tool use, and structured function/tool calling. All APIs use bearer-token authentication with an OPENAI_API_KEY. The official Node.js SDK (`openai`) and Python SDK (`openai`) are the primary client libraries. Rate limits vary by model and tier. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent performs: generating text, creating embeddings, producing images, building assistants, and using tools/functions.
body:
# OpenAI Ecosystem

OpenAI provides a suite of AI models accessible via a unified REST API. Agents use these models to generate text, create vector embeddings, produce images, build persistent assistants, and invoke structured tool calls. All endpoints live under `https://api.openai.com/v1/` and authenticate with a bearer token.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Generate text** — send system/user/assistant messages to the Chat Completions API and receive model-generated responses. See [[openai/chat]].
- **Create embeddings** — convert text into dense vector representations for similarity search, clustering, and RAG pipelines. See [[openai/embeddings]].
- **Produce images** — generate, edit, or create variations of images using DALL-E models. See [[openai/images]].
- **Build assistants** — create persistent AI assistants with threads, messages, and tool use via the Assistants API. See [[openai/assistants]].
- **Use tools/functions** — define JSON Schema tools that the model can invoke, enabling structured data extraction and external API integration. See [[openai/function-calling]].

## Models

OpenAI offers multiple model families optimized for different tasks:

| Model | Best For | Context Window |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| GPT-4o | General-purpose, multimodal | 128k tokens |
| GPT-4o-mini | Fast, cost-effective tasks | 128k tokens |
| o1 | Complex reasoning | 200k tokens |
| o3 | Advanced reasoning, code | 200k tokens |
| text-embedding-3-small | Embeddings (cost-effective) | 8,191 tokens |
| text-embedding-3-large | Embeddings (high accuracy) | 8,191 tokens |
| DALL-E 3 | Image generation | N/A |

See [[openai/models]] for a detailed comparison including pricing and capabilities.

## Libraries

- **openai (Node.js)** — official TypeScript/JavaScript SDK
- **openai (Python)** — official Python SDK
- **REST API** — direct HTTP calls to `https://api.openai.com/v1/`

## Authentication

All API requests require an `Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY` header. Create API keys at [platform.openai.com/api-keys](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys). Use environment variables — never commit keys to source control.

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
```

```javascript
import OpenAI from "openai";
const client = new OpenAI(); // reads OPENAI_API_KEY from env
```

sections.title: API overview
sections.body: The OpenAI API is organized around REST endpoints under `https://api.openai.com/v1/`. Key endpoints: `/chat/completions` (see [[openai/chat]]), `/embeddings` (see [[openai/embeddings]]), `/images/generations` (see [[openai/images]]), `/assistants` (see [[openai/assistants]]). All responses are JSON. Streaming is supported for chat completions via Server-Sent Events.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` as an environment variable. The official SDKs read it automatically. For direct HTTP, send `Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY`. Organization and project headers are optional: `OpenAI-Organization` and `OpenAI-Project`. Never commit keys to source control. Rotate keys at platform.openai.com/api-keys.

sections.title: Models overview
sections.body: GPT-4o is the flagship multimodal model for text and vision. GPT-4o-mini is optimized for speed and cost. o1 and o3 are reasoning models that think before answering. text-embedding-3-small and text-embedding-3-large produce vector embeddings. DALL-E 3 generates images from text prompts. See [[openai/models]] for detailed pricing and capability comparison.

sections.title: Rate limits
sections.body: Rate limits are per-organization and vary by model and tier. Limits are expressed in requests per minute (RPM), tokens per minute (TPM), and requests per day (RPD). Check your current limits at platform.openai.com/account/limits. Implement exponential backoff with retry on 429 responses. The `x-ratelimit-*` response headers show remaining quota.
